name: Makaha Valley
desc: Inland valley community behind Makaha Beach — small and quiet, surrounded by dramatic Waianae Range scenery.
sources: Leeward Coast community guides · Forum locals
insider: Kaneaki Heiau at the back of the valley is one of the best-restored ancient Hawaiian temples on Oahu — open for guided visits through Maili Community Association
The valley floor is sheltered from coastal winds and significantly calmer than beach-side Makaha — temperatures run a few degrees warmer but without the salt spray
The community is very small, perhaps 300-400 homes, and tight-knit — unfamiliar cars are noticed immediately and neighbors watch out for each other
Makaha Valley Plantation and the surrounding areas offer some of the most affordable single-family homes on Oahu, often under $450K
Wild chickens, feral cats, and occasional wild pigs are part of daily life — secure your trash and don't leave pet food outside
There is one road in and one road out of the valley — Makaha Valley Road connects to Farrington Highway, and that's your only option
The Waianae Mountain backdrop is the most dramatic valley scenery on Oahu — Mount Kaala, the island's highest peak at 4,025 feet, looms directly above
Cell service can be spotty in the deeper valley — T-Mobile and AT&T users report dead zones near the back
If you value isolation, natural beauty, and affordable living over convenience and amenities, Makaha Valley delivers like nowhere else on the island
The old Makaha Valley Country Club has been a source of community debate for years — check current status before expecting to play
